methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
d-Matrix, a Series C semiconductors company founded by Sid Sheth, has raised $450M to date against a current post-money valuation of $2.0B. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 3 public semiconductors peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-tech companies at similar or adjacent stages, including Etched, SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, Positron AI, and DEEPX.

Two valuation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C to the next financing round, implies a valuation of $3.4B. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-sector comparable companies—spanning stages from Series B through Series D—yields a median valuation of $2.7B, with a wide range from $529M (DEEPX) to $5.0B (Etched and SambaNova). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.3x across semiconductors) cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures for d-Matrix.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.3B to $3.9B, with a median of $3.0B. Key uncertainties include the significant dispersion within the peer set—a five-fold gap between floor and ceiling—and the unavailability of revenue data that would enable public market cross-checks. Stage-progression and peer multiples provide reasonable brackets, but stage heterogeneity among comparables limits precision.
